text.content:
Beat 4a \u2014 The Coastal Road

Act: Act 1 | Type: Montage Scene

Overview

The party has left Hrafnborg before dawn with forged papers and the King's supplies. Ahead: the Lantern Road north through frozen farmland, the treacherous Frostfen Marshes, and the refugee-choked Herdweg junction — roughly 100 miles and 4–5 days on failing roads through the Fimbulwinter.

This montage test covers the first leg of the journey to Stairwatch: from the city gates to the edge of Midgard's Shield foothills, before the Chainwarden Toll at Greypass.

Running This Scene

This is a narrative montage — a series of challenges the party faces as a group. Rather than a single roll, play this out as a sequence of scenes where each character contributes.

GM Guidance

  • Go around the table and ask each player how their character is helping.
  • Call for ability checks as appropriate — suggested modified [Resolute←15] (hard).
  • Let creative approaches succeed with good roleplaying, even without a roll.
  • Use the complications below as prompts — not every one needs to come up.
  • The goal is collaborative storytelling, not a pass/fail test.

Relevant Skills:

  • Strong
  • Quick or Discreet
  • Cunning or Cunning
  • Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant
  • Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive

Complications & Challenges

Use these as dramatic beats during the montage. Present them as situations the party must navigate together.

Round 1: The Lantern Road and the Frostfen

The road is failing. Mile-markers are buried or stolen. Chainwarden patrols demand papers from northbound travelers. Ahead, the Frostfen Marshes: thin ice over black water, a half-collapsed causeway, and a cold that feels wrong — wetter, heavier, like the land itself is exhaling.

  • Navigate the Lantern Road (Cunning or Cunning, modified [Resolute←15]) — Read buried markers, plot detours around collapsed sections, choose the right fork when the road splits.
  • Endure the cold and ration supplies (Strong, modified [Resolute←15]) — Resist frostbite, carry gear through snowdrifts, ford frozen streams. Supplies must last ten days.
  • Deceive early Chainwarden patrols (Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive, modified [Resolute←15]) — Talk past a roving patrol with forged papers; explain why you're heading north when everyone else goes south.
  • Scout safe passage through the Frostfen (Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant, modified [Resolute←15]) — Find the old causeway stones buried under frost. Avoid thin ice hiding black water beneath.
  • Navigate the Frostfen at night (Quick or Discreet, modified [Resolute←15]) — Cross the worst section after dark on ice-slick stones. Balance, leap tussocks, avoid the thin spots.
  • Read weather signs to avoid a squall (Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant, modified [Resolute←15]) — A Fimbulwinter squall drops visibility to arm's length in minutes. Read the cloud-patterns and wind-shift before it hits.

Escalation

If the scene needs more tension, introduce these additional complications:

Round 2: The Frostfen Margins and the Refugee Road

Beyond the worst of the marsh, the road meets the Herdweg — the old drovers' highway running east from the Interior Belt. The junction is choked with refugees fleeing south toward Hrafnborg. The party is heading north. Questions will be asked.

  • Find shelter in the Frostfen margins (Cunning or Cunning, modified [Resolute←15]) — The flat marshland offers no natural shelter. Locate an old peat-cutter's hut, an overturned barge, or reed-banks thick enough to break the wind.
  • Cross the Herdweg junction without attention (Presence or Agility) — Push north through southbound refugee flow without drawing questions. Why are healthy travelers heading toward the danger?
  • Resist marsh-sickness (Strong, modified [Resolute←15]) — The Frostfen's air carries something foul — not disease, but a heaviness in the lungs. A failed character wakes the next morning with a rattling cough (−1 to Might tests until a warm extended rest).
  • Avoid refugee columns (Presence or Agility) — Avoid drawing attention from desperate column families who may turn dangerous if they think you're hoarding supplies.
  • Calm a panicked pack-mule (Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive, modified [Resolute←15]) — The mule balks at a frozen corpse on the road and threatens to bolt with half the supplies.
  • Keep morale up at camp (Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive, modified [Resolute←15]) — Four days of grey-white emptiness and freezing wind. Keep each other from despair with stories, songs, small comforts.

Outcomes

Based on how the party performed, use the following as a guide:

Interlude: The Extinguished Shrine

By the roadside, a cairn of stacked stones stands waist-high. Its centre is hollow — a bowl where a flame should burn. The ash is cold. Dead wildflowers, frost-brittle, lie scattered around the base. The keystone is carved with a single runic word: Remember.

If any PC makes an offering (even just a breath warmed and placed in the basin), the keystone warms for just a moment, then goes cold again. Yggdrasil is listening — but too weak to answer.

No mechanical effect. This is a moment to feel the weight of what is failing.

Interlude: The Drowned Village

The road crosses what was once a shallow lake. Rooftops jut from the ice at the lake's centre. A market feet (2 m), a temple spire, streets and doorways — and movement below. Something with hands.

The PCs should not investigate. If they do: the ice creaks, and whatever is below tracks their movement. An easy Reason test identifies this as a draugr nest — the village died and the residents didn't leave.

If they move on: they gain a grim truth. The dead are everywhere. The Frostfen is a cemetery that doesn't know it's dead.

Total Success (5+ successes): 2 Milestones. Arrive at the Herdweg junction intact, supplies holding, morale manageable. Delay first Grafvitnir Pressure Check by one scene.

Partial Success (3–4 successes): 1 Victory. Arrive at cost — choose one: (1) −1 day's rations lost navigating Frostfen; (2) one PC has persistent cough (−1 Might tests until warm extended rest); or (3) a refugee recognised the King's seal on the papers (+1 to future Grafvitnir Pressure Checks).

Total Failure (2 failures before 5 successes): 0 Milestones. Arrive compromised: lose 2 days' rations, one PC has marsh-sickness, Grafvitnir agents know exact position (next Pressure Check triggers automatically at 5+). Director gains 3 Malice for the next scene.
